reasoning: As of the knowledge cutoff of November 9, 2025, there has been no World Health Organization (WHO) declaration of a new coronavirus pandemic. While a news report in February 2025 (Newsweek) highlighted the discovery of a bat coronavirus (HKU5-CoV-2) with potential for human spillover, subsequent research through November 2025 indicates this virus has not caused any humans outbreaks or significant health emergencies. WHO briefings and global news throughout 2025 have focused on the 'Pandemic Agreement' treaty, existing SARS-CoV-2 management, and other outbreaks like mpox and cholera, but no new coronavirus threat. Since the transition from a first report to a global pandemic declaration typically takes several months, and zero such movement has been seen by early November, the likelihood of a declaration in the remaining seven weeks of 2025 is extremely low.
